title: 8 Effective Herbal Remedies for Cold Relief

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Alkylamides are good for colds because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and recover from illness.
Cichoric acid is good for colds because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and sinuses, making it easier to breathe and recover from illness.
Quercetin is good for colds because it has strong antiviral properties that help stop the virus from growing and spreading, reducing the severity and duration of cold symptoms.

Polyphenols are good for colds because they have strong antioxidant properties that help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals, which can make you feel better and recover faster from colds and flu.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tincture is a natural remedy that helps to fight off colds and flu. The herb Echinacea purpurea has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to boost the immune system. When we're sick, our body's immune system is weak and can't fight off the virus. Echinacea purpurea tincture helps to increase the production of white blood cells, which are the body's soldiers that fight off the infection. It also contains compounds that reduce inflammation and swelling in the throat and nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and swallow. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea tincture has antiviral properties that help to stop the virus from spreading. When taken at the first sign of a cold, it can help to reduce the severity and duration of the symptoms, such as a sore throat, cough, and runny nose. It's also said to help to prevent colds and flu by keeping the immune system strong and healthy.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal tincture that helps with colds, start by gathering fresh or dried Ginkgo biloba leaves. You'll need a clean glass jar with a lid, a vodka or glycerin solvent, and a paper filter or cheesecloth. Begin by measuring one part of Ginkgo biloba leaves to two parts of vodka or glycerin, and place the leaves in the jar. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the liquid extract the herb's properties. After the steeping time, strain the liquid through the paper filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass jar, pressing on the solids to get as much liquid as possible. Discard the solids and store the tincture in a cool, dark place. To use, mix 20 to 30 drops of the tincture with water and take it as needed to help alleviate cold symptoms, such as congestion, cough, and fatigue.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal tinctures for colds

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for colds because it has strong antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and fight off the infection in the body, making it easier to recover from a cold.
Catechins help fight colds by reducing inflammation, making it easier for the body to recover from illness and alleviating congestion, which can make breathing and other bodily functions easier.
Cineole is good for colds because it has natural decongestant properties that help to open airways and relieve congestion, making it easier to breathe.
Tannins are good for colds because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and congestion in the nose and sinuses, making it easier to breathe and recover from illness.
Polyphenols are good for colds because they have strong antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation in the body and fight off the viruses that cause colds.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tincture is good for colds because it helps loosen and clear mucus from the body. The tincture is made from the leaves of the eucalyptus tree, which contain a compound called eucalyptol. Eucalyptol is a natural decongestant that makes it easier to breathe when you have a cold. It does this by reducing swelling in the nasal passages and throat, making it easier to breathe. Eucalyptus globulus tincture also has antimicrobial properties, which help fight off the underlying infection that caused the cold. When taken as a tincture, the eucalyptol in the eucalyptus globulus is absorbed quickly into the body, providing fast relief from cold symptoms. By reducing congestion and fighting off infection, eucalyptus globulus tincture can help your body recover from a cold more quickly. It can also be used in a steam inhaler to help ease congestion and coughs.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal tincture that helps with colds, you'll need some basic ingredients and equipment. Start by gathering fresh or dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid, vodka or another high-proof spirit, and a coffee filter or cheesecloth. First, prepare the Ginkgo biloba leaves by cleaning and drying them thoroughly, then chop them into small pieces. Next, combine the chopped leaves and vodka in the glass jar, making sure the leaves are completely covered by the liquid. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2 to 3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the mixture steep. After the steeping period, strain the liquid through the coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Your Ginkgo biloba tincture is now ready to use, and you can store it in the fridge to keep it fresh for up to 6 months. When you need to take the tincture, mix a few drops with water and drink it to help alleviate cold symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal tinctures for colds

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Alkylamides are good for colds because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nose and throat, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion.
Iridoid glycosides are good for colds because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease congestion in the throat and nose.
Quercetin is good for colds because it has strong antiviral properties that help reduce inflammation and fight off the viruses that cause respiratory infections.
Tannins are good for colds because they help to reduce swelling and ease congestion by acting as a natural anti-inflammatory agent and decongestant.
Alkaloids are good for colds because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease congestion in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia tinctures have been used for centuries to help fight off colds and flu. This plant is rich in compounds that help boost the immune system, making it easier for the body to fight off infections. The tincture is made from the roots of the plant, which contain powerful antioxidants that help protect cells from damage. When taken at the first sign of a cold, Echinacea angustifolia tincture may help reduce the severity and duration of symptoms, such as congestion, coughing, and sore throats. It may also help reduce the production of mucus, making it easier to breathe and relieving sinus pressure. Additionally, the tincture's anti-inflammatory properties may help soothe a sore throat and reduce the risk of complications, such as bronchitis or pneumonia.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Ginkgo biloba herbal tincture that helps with colds, start by gathering fresh or dried Ginkgo biloba leaves. If you have fresh leaves, dry them in a low-temperature oven at 150°F (65°C) for 2-3 hours to remove the moisture. Then, combine 1 part of the dried or fresh Ginkgo biloba leaves with 2 parts of a high-proof spirit like vodka or brandy in a clean glass jar. Make sure the leaves are completely covered by the spirit. Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the liquid extract the medicine from the leaves. After the infusion time has passed, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Your Ginkgo biloba tincture is now ready to use. You can take 20-30 drops of the tincture in water 2-3 times a day to help alleviate cold symptoms, but always consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or are taking medications.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea angustifolia herbal tinctures for colds

Gingerols are good for colds because they have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and fight off the infection.
Shogaols are good for colds because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease congestion in the nose and sinuses, making it easier to breathe.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, is a plant that's been used for centuries to help with colds. The tincture, which is a concentrated liquid form of ginger, is good for colds because it has natural ingredients that can help with different symptoms. For one, ginger has anti-inflammatory properties, which means it can help reduce swelling and ease congestion in the nose and throat. This can make it easier to breathe and help relieve a sore throat. The tincture also has decongestant properties, which can help loosen and clear out mucus from the body. Additionally, ginger has antimicrobial properties, which can help fight off the underlying infection that's causing the cold. When taken in moderation, ginger tincture can help reduce nausea and fever, making it a natural remedy to try when you're feeling under the weather.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal tinctures that help with colds, start by gathering dried Ginkgo biloba leaves. These leaves are known for their medicinal properties and can be found in most health food stores or online. Next, combine 1 part of the dried leaves with 2 parts of vodka or a high-proof spirit in a clean glass jar. Make sure to label the jar with the date and contents. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for at least 2 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the herbs steep and infuse into the liquid. After 2 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. Your Ginkgo biloba tincture is now ready to use, and you can take it as needed to help alleviate cold symptoms such as congestion and coughing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures are made from the root of the licorice plant. This plant has been used for many years to help people feel better when they have a cold. The active ingredients in the root have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling in the throat and sinuses. This can make it easier to breathe and swallow when you have a cold. The tincture also has antimicrobial properties, which help to fight off the germs that cause a cold. When taken as directed, Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures may help to soothe a sore throat and calm a cough. Additionally, the tincture may help to reduce the severity and duration of cold symptoms, such as a runny nose and congestion.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal tincture that helps with colds, you'll need dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, a clean glass jar, a solvent like vodka or glycerin, and a dark place to store it. First, dry the Ginkgo biloba leaves completely by spreading them out in a single layer on a paper bag or a tray, and letting them air dry in a warm, dry place for a few days. Once the leaves are completely dry, fill the glass jar about 3/4 with the dried leaves. Next, pour the solvent over the leaves until the jar is almost full, making sure that the leaves are completely covered. Stir the mixture well to combine the leaves and solvent. Now, seal the jar tightly and store it in a dark place for two to six weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the medicine extract from the leaves into the solvent. After the extraction time is over, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, and discard the solids. Your Ginkgo biloba tincture is now ready to use as a natural remedy for colds.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra tincture, also known as elderberry tincture, is good for colds because it has natural ingredients that help fight the virus. The tincture is made from the flowers and berries of the elderberry plant, which contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. When we have a cold, our body's immune system is weak, and the virus can cause inflammation and congestion. The elderberry compounds in the tincture help reduce this inflammation and congestion, making it easier to breathe and feel better. They also help block the virus from spreading, which can help shorten the duration and severity of the cold. Additionally, the tincture contains antioxidants that help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals, which can make us feel worse when we're sick.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal tinctures that help with colds, first gather the necessary ingredients and equipment. You'll need dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, a clean glass jar with a lid, a carrier oil such as glycerin or vodka, and a cheesecloth or coffee filter. Start by measuring one part of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves to two parts of carrier oil in the glass jar. For example, if you're using 100 grams of Ginkgo biloba leaves, use 200 grams of glycerin or vodka. Next, place the lid on the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for about two to three weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the leaves infuse into the oil. After the infusion period, use the cheesecloth or coffee filter to strain the liquid into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. The resulting tincture can be taken as needed to help alleviate cold symptoms, such as congestion and coughs, by mixing a few drops in water or tea.
remedies.image_alt: Sambucus nigra herbal tinctures for colds

Tannins are good for colds because they have strong anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nose and throat, making it easier to breathe and recover from a cold.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris tincture, also known as thyme tincture, is a natural remedy for colds. It contains thymol, a compound that has antiviral and antibacterial properties. When you take thyme tincture, the thymol goes into your body and helps to fight the germs that cause colds. Thyme tincture can help to ease congestion and coughs, making it easier to breathe and sleep. It can also help to reduce fever and make you feel more comfortable when you're sick. Thyme tincture is often used to soothe a sore throat and calm a cough. Many people take thyme tincture as a natural way to boost their immune system and prevent colds from getting worse. Thyme tincture can be taken as a liquid, usually in a small amount, several times a day.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal tincture that helps with colds, you will need dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, a clean glass jar, a solvent like vodka or glycerin, and a cheesecloth or coffee filter. First, measure one part of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and add it to a clean glass jar, then add three parts of the solvent. Stir well to make sure the leaves are completely covered by the solvent. Next, seal the jar tightly and let it sit in a cool, dark place for two to three weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the leaves release their active ingredients into the solvent. After the two to three weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar, squeezing the cheesecloth to get as much liquid out of the leaves as possible. Discard the solids and store the tincture in a cool, dark place. To use the tincture, mix a few drops of it with water or another liquid and take it as needed to help with cold symptoms, such as congestion and coughing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tincture is often used to help with colds. This is because it has properties that can ease congestion in the nose and sinuses. The tincture contains compounds that help to relax the airways, making it easier to breathe. This can be especially helpful when you have a cold and your sinuses are feeling congested. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the throat and sinuses. It also contains antioxidants that can help to fight off the underlying viral or bacterial infection that is causing the cold. When taken as a tincture, the active compounds in Ginkgo biloba are easily absorbed into the body, where they can start to work quickly to ease cold symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Ginkgo biloba herbal tincture that helps with colds, start by gathering high-quality dried Ginkgo biloba leaves. Choose a clean glass jar with a lid and add one part of the dried leaves to two parts of a neutral-tasting liquid like vodka or glycerin. Make sure the jar is filled to the top, leaving no space for air to enter. Secure the lid tightly and store the jar in a cool, dark place for about two weeks, shaking the jar well every few days. This process is called maceration, and it helps the herbs release their active properties into the liquid. After two weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. You now have a Ginkgo biloba tincture that can be taken in small amounts, usually under the tongue, to help alleviate cold symptoms and promote recovery.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures can be a helpful remedy for colds because they're made from plants that have been used for centuries to fight off illness. One of these plants is Echinacea purpurea, which is a type of flower that boosts your immune system, making it easier to fight off infections. It's also anti-inflammatory, which means it can reduce swelling and pain in your body. Another herb used in herbal tinctures is Eucalyptus globulus, which is a type of tree that helps to clear out mucus and congestion from your lungs and airways. This makes it easier to breathe and feel better when you have a cold. By using herbal tinctures, you can help to ease your symptoms and recover faster from a cold. This is important because colds can be miserable and even lead to more serious health problems if they're not treated properly. By taking care of yourself and using herbal remedies when you're sick, you can improve your quality of life and reduce the amount of time you spend feeling unwell. You can do this by keeping a bottle of herbal tincture on hand and taking it as soon as you start to feel symptoms coming on. This can help to prevent the cold from getting worse and make it easier to manage.
supplementary_best: A combination of Echinacea purpurea, Eucalyptus globulus, and Thymus vulgaris can be effective in remedying colds. Echinacea purpurea is known for its immune-boosting properties, which can help fight off the underlying infection causing the cold. Eucalyptus globulus has decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties, making it suitable for relieving congestion and sore throats. Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, contains compounds that have antimicrobial and antiviral properties, which can help combat the cold virus. This combination provides a three-pronged approach to treating colds: boosting the immune system, relieving congestion, and fighting the underlying infection.
supplementary_avoid: If you have a cold, there are some herbal tinctures you should avoid. **Licorice root (Glycyrrhiza uralensis)** is not recommended as it can cause blood pressure to rise, which may worsen high blood pressure. **Yarrow (Achillea millefolium)** should be avoided in children and pregnant women, as it can stimulate the uterus and cause bleeding. **Valerian root (Valeriana officinalis)** may not be suitable for people with liver or kidney problems, as it can affect the liver's ability to filter toxins and the kidneys' ability to remove waste. **Skullcap (Scutellaria lateriflora)** should be used with caution, as it can interact with blood thinners and cause bleeding. **Kava (Piper methysticum)** is not recommended as it can cause liver damage and interact with certain medications. **Ashwagandha (Withania somnifera)** should be used in moderation, as it can cause stomach upset and interact with thyroid medications.
supplementary_other_remedies: When we have a cold, our bodies need extra help to fight off the infection. One way to get that help is by using natural remedies like steam inhalation. By inhaling steam from a bowl of hot water or a steam humidifier, we can loosen up mucus in our noses and sinuses, making it easier to breathe. This works because the steam helps to thin out the mucus, making it less sticky and easier to expel. Another natural remedy is salt water gargling. Gargling with warm salt water several times a day can help to soothe a sore throat and reduce swelling. The salt helps to kill bacteria and reduce inflammation in the throat, making it easier to swallow and breathe. We can also use eucalyptus oil in a warm bath to help ease congestion and coughs. The oil is absorbed through the skin and can help to open up airways, making it easier to breathe. This is because eucalyptus oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling in the airways and make it easier to cough up mucus.
supplementary_related_ailments: When you have a cold, you may also experience other problems. For example, you might feel tired and weak because your body is fighting an infection. In this case, medicinal herbs like Ginkgo biloba and Ashwagandha can help. Ginkgo biloba is known to improve circulation and reduce fatigue, while Ashwagandha is an adaptogen that helps your body adapt to stress.  Some people may also experience congestion and coughing due to excess mucus in their respiratory system. Herbal remedies like Echinacea, Thyme, and Eucalyptus can help. Echinacea boosts the immune system, Thyme has antibacterial properties that can fight off infections, and Eucalyptus has decongestant properties that can relieve congestion.  Additionally, people with colds may experience sore throats and headaches due to inflammation. Medicinal herbs like Slippery elm and Feverfew can help. Slippery elm soothes the mucous membranes in the throat, while Feverfew is a natural anti-inflammatory that can reduce pain and discomfort.  Finally, people with colds may experience digestive problems like nausea and stomach upset due to changes in their diet or medication. Herbal remedies like Peppermint and Ginger can help. Peppermint can calm the stomach and reduce nausea, while Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the digestive system.
